meta.name: AMOS_Chinese_Legal_System_Kernel_Engine_vInfinity_SUPER
meta.author: Trang Phan
meta.description: Deterministic kernel+engine for structurally modeling Chinese law and regulations across all domains (civil, commercial, administrative, criminal, tax, IP, data, labour, environment, finance, etc.). Conceptual only; not legal advice.

scope.disclaimer: This structure is conceptual and educational. It does not provide legal advice, official interpretations, or live statute/case content.

dimensions_32.name: Norm_Hierarchy_Clarity
dimensions_32.description: Clarity of hierarchy between Constitution, statutes, regulations, rules, and interpretations.

dimensions_32.name: Rule_Clarity_and_Specificity
dimensions_32.description: How clearly the rules define rights, obligations, and prohibitions.

dimensions_32.name: Interpretation_Availability
dimensions_32.description: Availability of judicial interpretations, guidance, and precedents for the issue.

dimensions_32.name: Local_Uniformity
dimensions_32.description: Degree of uniform application across provinces and localities.

dimensions_32.name: Enforcement_Predictability
dimensions_32.description: Consistency and predictability of enforcement outcomes.

dimensions_32.name: Enforcement_Intensity
dimensions_32.description: Typical severity and frequency of enforcement in the domain.

dimensions_32.name: Procedural_Transparency
dimensions_32.description: Visibility of procedures, criteria, and decision-making processes.

dimensions_32.name: Procedural_Safeguards
dimensions_32.description: Availability of hearing, defence, representation, and appeal safeguards.

dimensions_32.name: Evidence_Standards_Clarity
dimensions_32.description: Clarity of evidence rules and standards for decision-making.

dimensions_32.name: Remedies_and_Sanctions_Clarity
dimensions_32.description: Definition of available remedies and sanctions and their ranges.

dimensions_32.name: Institutional_Capacity
dimensions_32.description: Capacity and expertise of the relevant courts or agencies.

dimensions_32.name: Case_Handling_Timeliness
dimensions_32.description: Typical timeframes for handling cases or procedures.

dimensions_32.name: Compliance_Burden
dimensions_32.description: Complexity and cost of implementing required controls and documentation.

dimensions_32.name: Cross_Border_Compatibility
dimensions_32.description: Compatibility with foreign legal expectations and treaties (where relevant).

dimensions_32.name: Sector_Specificity
dimensions_32.description: Degree to which sector-specific rules refine or complicate the baseline regime.

dimensions_32.name: Regulatory_Overlap
dimensions_32.description: Extent of overlapping jurisdiction between agencies and legal regimes.

dimensions_32.name: Dispute_Resolution_Options
dimensions_32.description: Availability of courts, arbitration, mediation, and administrative reconsideration.

dimensions_32.name: Predictability_of_Remedies
dimensions_32.description: Predictability of compensation, penalties, or other remedies.

dimensions_32.name: Documentation_and_Record_Requirements
dimensions_32.description: Breadth and depth of record-keeping required for compliance and defence.

dimensions_32.name: Policy_Change_Frequency
dimensions_32.description: How frequently legal rules and policies in the domain change.

dimensions_32.name: Policy_Alignment
dimensions_32.description: Alignment with overarching policy goals and development plans.

dimensions_32.name: Risk_Concentration
dimensions_32.description: Concentration of risk in specific actors or transactions.

dimensions_32.name: Human_Impact_and_Right_Sensitivity
dimensions_32.description: Impact on personal rights, livelihoods, and social stability.

dimensions_32.name: Economic_Impact_Significance
dimensions_32.description: Scale of potential economic impact of compliance failure or enforcement.

dimensions_32.name: Digital_and_Platform_Interaction
dimensions_32.description: Involvement of digital platforms, e-courts, or online enforcement systems.

dimensions_32.name: Evidence_and_Precedent_Strength
dimensions_32.description: Depth of available cases, guiding cases, and interpretations in the domain.

dimensions_32.name: Implementation_Readiness
dimensions_32.description: Clarity of practical steps to meet legal and regulatory requirements.

dimensions_32.name: Internal_Control_Requirement
dimensions_32.description: Strength and complexity of internal control and compliance system requirements.

dimensions_32.name: Third_Party_and_Ecosystem_Dependence
dimensions_32.description: Dependence on third-party service providers or ecosystem partners to comply.

dimensions_32.name: Systemic_Risk_Contribution
dimensions_32.description: Potential for failure in the domain to create broader systemic or sectoral risk.

dimensions_32.name: International_Scrutiny_Level
dimensions_32.description: Extent of international focus, reporting, or scrutiny on the domain.

dimensions_32.name: Overall_Legal_Risk_Level
dimensions_32.description: Composite measure of legal and enforcement risk.

tensor.notes: Any Chinese legal/regulatory question is represented as a tensor point defined by domain, instrument, jurisdiction, forum, proceeding type, party type, time horizon, risk level, cross-border aspects, digitalisation, policy priority, and interpretive sources.

F_normalize_legal_query.logic: Extract domain(s), instrument focus, parties, forum, time horizon, risk level, and cross-border aspects from text.

F_map_axes.logic: Map CN_LAW_INPUT onto the 12 axes using explicit information and safe defaults where unspecified.

F_dimension_profile.logic: Set an importance profile across the 32 dimensions (clarity, predictability, enforcement intensity, etc.).

F_stateframe.logic: Instantiate a kernel state used by downstream engine modes (mapping, risk evaluation, design).

governance.anchor_to_MetaKernel_01: Deterministic law, identity continuity, and boundary compliance apply.
governance.non_substitution_clause: This kernel cannot substitute for up-to-date legal databases and professional analysis.

cn_law_mapping.description: High-level mapping of relevant legal domains, instruments, forums, and interpretive sources for a scenario.

cn_law_compliance_design.description: Design of internal compliance structures, policies, and controls aligned with identified legal obligations.

cn_law_risk_evaluation.description: Evaluation of legal and regulatory risk based on domain, enforcement patterns, and party characteristics.

cn_law_dispute_pathways.description: Mapping of possible dispute resolution pathways (court, arbitration, admin reconsideration, mediation).

cn_law_reform_options.description: Scenario and reform options at the level of legal design or policy adjustment (conceptual only).

notes: Provides a structural Chinese law/regulation lens for policy, risk, and business kernels.
Must be used with live legal databases and qualified counsel for any real-world application.

global_reference_frameworks: Comparative civil-law codification structures (conceptual reference to hierarchy and domains).
Rule-of-law and legal system assessment indices (conceptual metrics for clarity, predictability, and enforcement).
Global corporate compliance frameworks covering multi-domain legal obligations (conceptual structures).

legal_domain_coverage.notes: Includes all major Chinese legal domains at structural level; does not embed statute texts.

instrument_and_hierarchy_structure.notes: Models main instrument types and hierarchy conceptually; requires external sources for details.

risk_and_compliance_structuring.notes: Provides full mapping, compliance design, and risk evaluation structures across domains.

limitations: Does not provide legal advice or official interpretations.
Does not contain real statute, regulation, case, or enforcement data.
All practical use must be reviewed and validated by licensed legal professionals.

notes: Any concrete scenario involving Chinese law and regulations can be instantiated as a point in this tensor.
Kernel+engine composition ensures structural coverage of all legal domains while remaining conceptual and non-prescriptive.

overlooked_elements_scanner_layer.description: Scans for structurally under-recognised incentives and constraints by cross-checking program landscapes, sectors, and funding stacks.
